reviews.review_text: This is my second turn based game after expedition 33, and we are now two for two on goated games. The life-sim gameplay and palace exploration were both really fun and engaging. I have never encountered a game with the calendar system before and really enjoyed it. Planning out what I was going to do with my week in the game was really fun. The art style and music really shine in this game, and I can't wait to play Persona 3 Reload next.

FYI - got like 250 hours on PS5

The art direction is amazing, the game is amazing, the characters are amazing, Morgana is... The music is amazing and the story is amazing too!

Overall it's one of the best games I've ever played. For how long it is (100-130 hours), the quality is insanely high throughout most of it. Very strongly recommend.

Also I won't say why, but max out Maruki's social link as soon as possible. It unlocks what is by far the best content in the game.
♥♥♥♥♥ peak, and finally as a persona fan, i actually play the game. no more larp

Heard about P5R through Google recommendations. People kept calling it one of the best games ever made, a masterpiece. When I first played it, the music, graphics, and gameplay were exactly as great as I expected. I listened to the soundtrack countless times, fought difficult bosses with my own strategies, and somehow the gameplay never got boring.

But the thing that truly made this game special for me was the story.

I didn’t expect to get so attached to the characters and the everyday moments. This game taught me a lot, from going to school, improving the quality of my life, socializing with people, learning different principles about life, and many more things I didn’t expect to take from a video game.

Yeah, it’s long as hell, but by the end, I genuinely didn’t want it to end. It wasn’t just a game for me. It felt like a whole chapter of my life.

P5R really changed the way I see the world.

Persona 5 Royal was my first Japanese RPG, and all I can say is: wow, wow, wow.

What impressed me most was the incredible depth of the game. At its core, Persona 5 Royal is about friendship and the connections you build with other people. If you don't enjoy reading dialogue, this probably isn't the game for you. But for me, all of that character development is exactly what made the experience so immersive. The relationships feel surprisingly fresh and most major characters are interesting in their own way.

I genuinely struggled to put this game down and ended up spending far more hours on it than I originally intended. The gameplay loop is incredibly addictive. Once you start playing, real-life time passes incredibly fast. The game encourages you to carefully manage your in-game days and take your time, but before you know it, you've spent hours completely immersed in its world.

For new players, my biggest recommendation is: don't look up too much online. There are spoilers everywhere, and the game is clearly designed to be explored at your own pace. The freedom to make your own choices is a huge part of its charm. You don't need to min-max everything to finish the game, although it's possible to miss some bonus content if you make certain decisions along the way.

Another thing that deserves special praise is the soundtrack. It's absolutely fantastic. I downloaded it after finishing the game and still listen to it regularly.

A fair warning: this is a very long game and that won't appeal to everyone. The story was surprisingly good, written from the perspective of teenagers while still being engaging and relatable for adults like myself. And when it all comes together, the payoff feels earned. After spending so much time with these characters and sharing so many experiences with them, saying goodbye is genuinely bittersweet.

One of the best gaming experiences I've had.

t's my first time playing a game in the series.

Story? Fine. Keeps you interested. The "adults are so ♥♥♥♥♥♥ and horrible" theme eventually had me rolling my eyes.

Gameplay? Enjoyable. Mechanics are fun. Capturing personas and merging them got old. I had zero interest in that aspect of gameplay. The Palace infiltration keeps you pushing through and engaged but became tedious. The puzzles scattered throughout the palaces weren't bad but they also became annoying because I just wanted to finish the palace without the puzzles slowing me down.

School life: It was fine. Initially I was interested in hearing all the dialogue and running around doing everything but 6 months later I'm skipping dialogue and getting through each day in 2 minutes.

Story again: Too much damn talking. I was skipping dialogue A LOT towards the end. I basically understood the gist of what was going down almost immediately and the conversations generally become corny time wasters.

Weird: Why the hell are we stealing the heart of pedo only for Joker's teacher to date him? But since it's all these adult women after Joker it's fine? Weird messaging there. The phantom thieves are teenagers. Why are these teenage girls so sexualized? It's weird to play this game as an adult and watch all this play out.

Weird cont: the penis head persona at the end of the game? Why. Just why.

Deductions: 1 for gameplay getting old, 1 for the corny dialogue, 1 for the weird pervy stuff.

Rating: 7/10

I didn't waste my time but I think I'm good on the Persona series if this is what it aims to be as a game. I don't recommend it, despite the quality of the game.

It is astounding how every minute of this very long game keeps you engaged, not a single moment feels like a waste, and I believe that is Persona 5 Royal’s biggest strength.

With its amazing characters, highly structured and stylish combat, and deeply moving writing and the MUSIC, the game has utmost to no flaws. Everything combined feels masterfully made.

My favorite part was the Royal-exclusive Third Semester, which takes an already phenomenal game and elevates it to something genuinely insightful and moving.

It is close to, if not completely, a masterful game
I spent 100 hours on this game for one playthrough. This was a life-changing experience and now I see everything differently, even in real life.

Once in a lifetime experience. If you're planning to get this game, just do it when it's on sale. The game gets progessively better once you get past the tutorial phase.

So much character developments from all of the casts. Especially pancake lover >:) You feel really attracted to them despite being a 2D/3D sprites. Everyone has their flaws, quirks and moments which makes them feel more humane. I like Yusuke, Iwai, Maruki, Ryuji, Yoshida and Kasumi's confidant the most.

Mundane tasks turned into something I enjoy doing. Everything you do in this game has a purpose, except of sleeping of course. 

This game made dungeon-crawling fun for me. The villains has their own custom dungeon and a randomly-generated dungeon for grinding purposes. I'm the type of guy to enjoy grinding endlessly so any kinds of dungeon works for me. Someone from Atlus also added an option to have mercy at the monsters you're battling (only fun in the first playthrough).

Persona? It's more than a goon-bait, it's about teenagers fighting against a corrupt society and the chains of oppression. Of course they're gonna do stupid stuff such as blackmailing a certain someone for lewd drawings. But, the fact that a teenager has a **very detailed** proportions of boobies kinda contradicts my part :|



Tips: Get Ryuji's confidant to lvl 7 at the beginning to make Mementos (random dungeon) so much easier to grind. I did Ann's first and... Yeah, I wasted a lot of time.
Alsooo, you can teleport to other safe rooms while inside a safe room. Yes, I've wasted at least 4 hours just from travelling from the beginning to the end of a palace.
Lastly, do the politician confidant in Station Square (Shibuya). He ranks up everytime and is only available during Sundays (Apart from obtaining his arcana during weekdays. You must talk to him, work at beef bowl shop. Next day, talk to him again and work at beef bowl shop again. Following day, talk to him. You can obtain the job by signing up in the Underground Walkway job magazines). He lets you abuse demon's mercy for extra money. By extra, I mean like an entire bank.

Persona 5 is one of the most relevant games of the past 20 years.  If you have ever enjoyed a JRPG you owe this one to yourself.  This places a crunch hardcore RPG experience against what we now call a visual novel.  The game is half made up of narrative and you play your character's roll in daily modern life while going through what equates to a top tier manga.

That said, I'm glad I got to experience the original version because I don't really like anything they did with Royale.  The og was master course in achieving amazing effects through efficient use of limited resources.  The new one might as well be on an unreal engine.  Still a perfect game and you probably won't notice the difference if you didn't play it before.

If you told me the 12$ game was gonna be this entertaining, fun, life-changing and provide so many hours while leaving me wanting for more, I would've doubted you.
10/10 a true one of a kind
PS: performance was dogass, gracias denuovo for the amazing 20fps in crowded areas
Got into this game finally even tho I already completed P2-4 after seeing some doomed yaoi edit back in December 2025 and they weren't kidding. That brown haired twink is a skin craver.

An amazing cast of characters.
Addictive gameplay.
Some of the best music you will find in gaming.
And an emotional story that makes you feel sad to say goodbye when it comes to an end.

The first arc is a bit of a drag to get through but once you get into the game and new characters start getting introduced its impossible to put down.

Also one of the characters is genuinely one of the best written autistic characters I have seen.

If you play this game to completion I am certain you won't regret it.

I came to this series late, later than most people I know at least, and I never expected it to mean what it means to me now. I had touched the other Persona games before. I spent some hours inside them, but I always drifted away before the credits could roll. Something always pulled me out before the story could finish what it started in me. This time was different. This was the first one I stayed with until the very end and it left a mark I cannot wipe away.

When I think about the gameplay, I have nothing left to ask for. Every system, every rhythm of the days and nights, it all fit together in a way that felt complete. When I think about the story, I have even less to ask for. And yet neither of those things is the real reason I am sitting here writing this. This is a story about us. About being human. About the young heart that looks out at a broken world, sees clearly what is wrong with it, and aches to change something before it even understands how.

The game taught me something I should have already known but needed to be reminded of. You cannot do it alone. It begins with yourself, with the courage to look honestly at your own life, and then it grows into something larger only when other people choose to stand beside you. That is when the world finally softens enough to be moved. I kept asking myself a simple question as I played. What would a high schooler actually do, dropped into situations this heavy, this unfair, this much bigger than they are. I watched the story walk straight into the worst corners of our reality, the cruelty hiding behind polite smiles, the adults who choose to look away, the silence that shields the guilty and abandons the hurt.

And still, the characters got up. Still, they faced the next morning, and the one after that. That is not a fantasy. That is the most honest thing this game ever said to me. This is how life is. You meet the worst of it, you carry the weight of it, and you keep moving anyway, because somewhere along the way you realize stopping was never truly an option.

I know it is just a game. I keep telling myself that, the way you repeat a thing you do not entirely believe. Because when I look back at the time I spent inside it, what it gave me does not feel small at all. It pointed gently at the ordinary parts of my own life. The friendships I take for granted. The slow, unremarkable days. The chances I almost let slip past me without noticing. And it asked me, kindly, to hold them closer while they are still mine to hold.

I am thankful I stumbled into this one. I did not plan to love it. I did not plan for a game to feel like a quiet, honest conversation with myself in a dark room. And now I sit here, feeling a little grateful, hoping the rest of this series finds me someday the same way this one did. Hoping they ask me the same tender questions and trust me to answer them honestly.

Maybe arriving late to something is its own small mercy. You reach it exactly when you are ready to be changed, and not a single moment before.

Oh man, what a ride this game has been; this game is jam-packed with content to the brim. There is no doubt this game is a long experience, and coming from a visual novel fan myself, it is one of the best games I've ever played. 

It is impossible to achieve 100% completion in this game without a guide; I gave up trying to play without one in the middle of the semester. HOO BOY, Okimura's Palace is the hardest in my experience, absolutely unforgiving in its bosses and puzzles. To think that Atlus would learn their lesson, but no, they did that to Maruki's palace too, lol. 

PS: Somehow got the best ending in the game.
I really enjoyed this game the social link, the battle, the music it's so freaking Good I'm halfway through, and definitely want to finished it

Being the longest game in the Persona series you be led to believe that burn out could become an issue with some playthroughs taking upwards of 150+ hours, I'm here to say that doesn't matter this game doesn't just have and amazing story but it is constantly making sure you have something to do so you never get board. Persona 5 Royal really is an amazing game although very easy is still an absolute blast and i never found my self not liking any of the characters and their progression as their "character" develops. Compared to P3R in a story sense it is fairly tame although it still challenges some very dark topics, it doesn't drive them home like P3R. The story as a whole is amazing and well worth getting through with the gameplay like all Personas feeling just right. After 100% the game i can say this is well worth your time and if you struggled with the grind in P3R this will be a breath of fresh air as it gives you some breathing room when hunting them achievements and getting through the story. Don't hesitate just buy and give it a good go. :)
Love the story, the music, the gameplay, the character design, and visual. Also big fan of turn base game

This gaming is crashing so often that i stuck in same 15mins level upto 3hrs im sick of it if ur pc dosent go well with denuvo drm you will be freaked.
[Game works fine after under volting and i luv it now]

ONE OF THE GREATEST GAMES I HAVE EVER PLAYED IN MY LIFE!

I have played many great games in my life but this game right here has blown me away in a myriad number of ways and will always hold a special place in my heart which is still saying less for what a true work of art it is (Yuske would be proud). Yeah that's right those bold letters at the top mean business for i will further go in detail and lay down all the reasons why you need to play this game right now if you haven't. Also heads up the review is sort of spoiler-free (atleast for the most part) so read on if you find my thoughts interesting.

This review is definitely going to be a long one so buckle in as there's lots to unpack. 
Fifth installment in the series and yet it's largely self-contained. You do not need to play any previous games as this was my first ever Persona too.

Ok i will address the elephant in the room right away which is the soundtrack. This game has some of the greatest music in gaming that i have ever experienced. Like every new scenario, or encounter, every unique boss fight etc. has it's own beat. Every moment in the game is a joy because of the tunes running in the back be it the soothing version of "Beneath the mask" when it's raining or the heart pumping "Last Surprise" during combat. Shogi Meguro my well have literally been on fire when he was composing the absolute life out of these songs. The vocalist Lynn Inaizumi has an angelic voice, everytime i heard her lyrics i just started smiling, it's simply amazing. Might be the only game where i actively found myself bouncing in the chair to every piece of music.

This game i believe easily checkboxes almost all the pillars of what makes a good videogame. However this is a story and dialogue driven turn based RPG which unfortunately happens to be a no-no for a bunch of people out there who prefer having complete control over their actions. To all my dudes and dudettes who are hesitant to get into this genre due to fear of succumbing to boredom or finding turn based not engaging enough i solemnly make a promise that this game right here is the pinnacle of it's category. While Expedition 33 was my first ever, this game sort of beats it imo due to the vast amounts of content it has.

You play as a troubled young guy who is just trying to be competent in a society littered with all manner of harassment, crime and cruelty. He helps a potential victim and accidentally taps into abilities that he never dreamt of before and in exchange puts alot of heat on himself. Now he has to prove his "existence" and "worth" in a society fraught with said various injustices. He has to work his way from the bottom and become a rising star. On one hand you are surviving reality as a highschooler, trying to meet your social expectations, on the other you are fighting forces of cognition in an alternate reality as a vigilante. These cognitive realities are in the shape of palaces which you then infiltrate (my personal favorite being the fourth one since it had the best music and one of my favourite confidants). Going into further detail here will drop me in spoiler territory so take my word for it when i say that the story will stay with you for a long time and may even change your life, the sudden unexpected twists and turns hit wonderfully. Being able to shift between two different perspectives for the same character is so engaging and a breath of fresh air. It's like playing two games at the same time. And then you also have the animated cutscenes which are cherry on top.

Do you happen to be a fan of lore and dialogue? Yes? Then you have arrived at the right place. No seriously you will casually let yourself loose for hours in the mountain peaks of lore dump (through simple things like solving crossword puzzles, watching the TV or paying attention in class etc.) and long engaging conversations with your friends and other social peers in the form of Confidants which you level up and benefit from. You actively take part in the dialogue exchanges and are given choices that reflect your character so make sure you choose wisely. Not only that but there's a ton of different stuff you can do throughout the game and not a single moment feels wasted or redundant. Tokyo is breathing with life here with gossip running in the back and different things happening in the public all the time. You can listen in on nearly everyone in the vicinity be it a pedestrian or a Shujin academy student and learn alot about what people are upto helping you discover new things.

Essentially you have three different ways of spending your day where you can do daily activities like working a part-time job, reading and studying, watching movies or taking care of yourself etc. Then you have your palaces alongside another area which you unlock later on for grinding and leveling up your personas and tackling any requests and lastly you have your Confidant system where you hangout, unlock new abilities and strengthen your relationships. The game actively gives you a reason to involve yourself in all these activities by helping you level up your social stats.

Wanna rizz up ladies and look cool while talking to them? Yeah work on your Charm, read stuff related to it, workout your body, take medicated baths that occur only on a certain day of the week. Wanna sound shrewd and intellectual during a conversation? Make sure you answer everything correctly, learn about different things through magazines or books and level up your Knowledge. Wanna do something risky and daring? Ensure you have enough Guts to do that and so on.
 
Visually the game looks spectacular. It has a unique art style and gives off a 3D anime vibe. From mere things like loading screens to new area transitions it packs in the feeling of being really there standing amidst the crowd. I like how they added a nice touch of walking right through npcs, which not only acts as a visual philosophy (you are like a nobody in the world, just another ordinary person, striving to make a name and living) but also as a gameplay convenience since Tokyo is bustling with life.
 
Some simple tips, the game features a calendar system of progression, meaning you have deadlines to complete things that you will not be able to do so on a later date. This may sound intimidating at first to some because people prefer to relax and take their time with everything but there was always more than enough time and i would have extra days to relax and work on my confidants. Because the individual days function in a static sort of sense where you can run around for as long as you want during that day and complete anything that won't drastically set the time forward which basically includes short interactions like making purchases etc. Also try to complete palaces in one in-game day, i would suggest not pulling yourself out midway, always secure the infiltration route and then tackle the boss anyday before the deadline.

I highly recommend this game as it's a foundational stepping stone for anyone new to turn-based jrpgs. I hope my ceaseless raving/advertising/review was able to convince you in some way because this game right here is one for many ages to come.

Wish I could erase my memories just to play it for the first time all over again. Story still hits hard with its themes, in 2026 more so than ever. what can i say? 300 hours here + 475 on switch, and I haven't regretted a second of it. banger soundtrack, compelling narrative, enjoyable characters, awesome turn-based gameplay, excellent level design, and it's made me either tear up or straight up sob every time i finish the game. it's very special to me.
don't forget those confidants omfg anyways this game was beautiful and so fun to play through shuake (and yusuke) you mean the world to me COME BACK TO ME

[b] Massive overrated game [/b]. The fans are overlooking the huge flaws that makes this game painful to play. I list them below:

[u]Biggest offender[/u]
The writing, never have I seen such a sloppy work. The repetions of already known information is unbelivable. Almost as if the writters couldn't be bothered to read through their own slop once to rework the writing to make it interesting and engaging. I kid you not the game would be 1/3 shorter without the repetition. The problem gets worse by the fact, that every minor detail that happens in the game is pointed out by the game, leaving nothing for the player to discover. 

[u]episodic structure[/u]
game consists of 5 or 6 episode that makes the story feel stiff and artifisal

[u]npc[/u]
npcs feel in no way alive and self-thinking. They all have the same template arc.

[u]mc[/u]
0 emotions, always cool

Tl:Dr: you will miss nothing out if you pass on this game. If you haven't played already get Clair Obscur: Expeditoin 33, its better in every way.

Most of the time, before playing a game, id look into it beforehand. Persona 5 was an exception, I didn't know what to expect. But, after playing, I realised that I was hooked, addicted, i'd spent hours straight playing it even during exam season. 

Everything about the game felt immersive and enjoyable, from the battle system to the storyline, everything. The iconic soundtrack, the interesting characters, and even the experience of living a normal highschool life inside the game, it was all so much fun. 

9.5/10
Masterpiece. Appreciate the mundane and evrerything.

Denuvo causes the game to be unplayable on the Steam Deck if it's not connected to the internet.  When trying to launch, a message will tell you to put the Deck in 'Online Mode' first.  This is unacceptable for a single-player game and worthy of a refund.  I don't recall having this issue when I first purchased the game.
This game has everything history, mechanics, etc. It will keep you entertained long time

This is the time of game that will genuinely change your life the first time you finished it. 
I introduce you, Persona 5 Royal! One of my first and also one of the best JRPG that I've ever played! 
The playtime speak for itself, by the time I wrote this review, I have 122 hours of Persona 5 Royal, I decided to dig myself deeper into the game by trying to get 100% achievement on Steam. Never fully finished the game until you get 100% am I right? 10/10 best games I ever bought.

Persona 5 Royal is one of the greatest games I've ever played in my whole life, This game Has a unique art style, Great soundtrack, Gameplay, Social Links with the characters , If you want a long journey that makes you enjoy everything you could imagine in a video game, Persona 5 Royal is THE ONE

After spending 100+ hours in this game and finally hitting that 100% achievement mark, I can honestly say Persona 5 Royal is a masterpiece. It's a massive time investment, but every single hour was worth it.

10/10.

150 hours of fun and emotions accompanied by such a satisfying and fulfilling ending. The Royal expansion brings a perfect closure to the story that left no unanswered questions and no holes to be filled. Thank you, Atlus, for this experience

This game is one of those rare games that leaves you feeling genuinely depressed when it's over because you don't want it to end. That has only happened to me twice before, with Persona 4 Golden being the other example.

What can I say that hasn't already been said about this game? It certainly has some flaws, but I don't think it's wrong to call it essentially a 10/10 experience. The characters, story, music, and overall presentation come together in a way that few games can match.

A couple of tips for new players:

* If you want a smoother experience, consider downloading a mod that skips the intro logos and opening scenes. It saves some time when launching the game.
* On Linux (at least on my desktop PC, not a Steam Deck), the game can sometimes crash after resuming from system suspend if online features are enabled. To avoid this, I recommend turning the online functions off before suspending your system.

Overall, Persona 5 Royal is an unforgettable experience and easily one of the best games I've ever played.

Persona 5 is in my humble opinion the best in the series. It made me cry, gave me chills, and kept me engaged in my 80+ hours. This is not the first time I've played it, and it won't be the last. Please play this game if you have any interest. If you find it on sale all the better.
I've played this game 3 times now, if you include the original. Its incredible, not flawless, but truly something special if you enjoy narrative heavy games and turn-based combat.

this review will contain spoilers for the story of the game so be aware.

This is the first persona game i played and got "Perfect Game" on and i can say that this game is not without flaws, especially on the story side some moments didn't stand right with me like: 
[spoiler] the choices you make throughout the game don't matter much on the ending of the game, there's only one confidant you need to max out in order to unlock new game content and the "true ending". Also the ending decision is quite literally what you choose to say in one cutscene with the game pointing out when you're choosing the "bad ending" response and tells you "are you sure this is what you want to choose?" that kind of makes even those choices useless unless you're trying to watch the bad ending on purpose, maybe I'm asking too much but it would be cool if the decisions mattered throughout the game for different endings like in Detroit Become Human for example. I also didn't understand in the Sae Niijimas interrogation moment, where you choose the ending you want to go for, how choosing not to give away your friends names changes the ending, I'm pretty sure joker, under the work of the truth serum said all of their names, because at least Makotos name was said and was a big plot twist for Sae, my only guess is that Sae wanted to hear them from Joker without the work of truth serum but i don't know what she expected to change. Also the true ending of third semester, THEY DID NOT HAVE TO SHOW AKECHI BROUGHT BACK TO LIFE. LET HIM REST IN PEACE!!! (P.S. To be fair we never see him die and monas cognitive version also died but he didn't so maybe it's logical) I liked the true ending and how there were these government agents in the black car following the phantom thieves, but c'mon did they really have to do that with Akechi just to milk some more out of the phantom thieves in the future? Also i found the gameplay of the battling system a bit boring and repetitive so by the end of the game i just used the most powerful dlc persona, right now I'm playing persona 3 reload and i think the battle gameplay is better there, but they're both my first games in the turn-based combat style games and i played like just one third of persona 3. [/spoiler]

what did i like about the game?
I liked the slow pace and length of the game and because of it you get very attached to the characters and care about them, there were some emotional moments where i was on the verge of tears from happiness or sadness, especially because i feel like the characters are relatable to everyone to some point, they're written very well and all have their character development and tragic backstory. [spoiler] (my favorite palace on emotional impact was Futabas palace because of how much i could see myself in her). [/spoiler] The fighting system was decent but i don't have decent opinion on it since it's my first turn-based combat game but the showtimes and all out attacks and their banners are incredibly cool, overall the style of the game is amazing, as well as the music, if i had to describe this game in three words i would say "stylish, good sounding, emotional" oh, that's four words. Also the game reminded me of undertale because of it's characters and how you can spend time with them to get closer to them and get buffs, or spare shadows. Second awakenings are also interesting and it's fun to think of what could their persona transform to once you max them out, sometimes in the game you will also have to complete your confidants requests in order to progress in the confidant rank and that's a cool way to give you a reason to explore [spoiler] momentos. [/spoiler] I really enjoyed the game and i'm a fan now. See you later alligator i go play Persona 3 Reload now!

While the premise of a bunch of high-schoolers getting superpowers to fight evil may sound trite and cliché, Persona 5 is much more awesome than you might have thought at first. Coupled with amazing soundtrack, sleek visuals, intriguing story and very fast-paced turn-based combat it can easily consume your life for weeks. I also wish to high-light the Royal part of the story as it was really well-written and touching and gives me more hope for Persona 6. Now, the first flaw I can name is that this game is really text-heavy and much of it is fluff, although by the virtue of knowing Japanese and loving visual novels, it was pretty digestible in my case, plus there is a skip button. The second flaw is that the hard difficulty wasn't really that hard, so at some point I just stopped using fused personas altogether. Honestly, if you are the target audience of this game, you probably know and love it. Cheers.

Just finally fully completed the game, all achievements and story related things are completed (excluding thieves den since that's a whole different problem). I can strongly say and recommend someone to play Persona 5 Royal now. I genuinely believe that P5R is the greatest game to ever be made. Whether it be from the stunning graphical design to the phenomenal soundtrack, this game manages to check every box. The story is strong and emotional, the characters and well thought out and have depth to them, the gameplay is interesting and won't leave you feeling bored. Throughout this entire ~85 hour playthrough, this game has managed to capture my attention, never leaving me wanting more or feeling bored. While my only gripe is the DLC personas sort of ruining the challenge of the game, and Okumaru giving me a mental breakdown, this game is a masterpiece. 10/10

Persona 5 Royal is an enhanced re-release including all DLC from the original, gameplay changes, and major story additions. It is without a doubt a masterpiece, and as someone who plays lots of JRPGs, this game sits among my favourites. I will not reiterate things most people have said already, but everything from the music, gameplay, art style, and story is phenomenal. Despite having played Persona 5 vanilla and Royal on my PS4, I enjoyed my third playthrough on Steam almost as much as the first time I experienced the game.

Compared to vanilla Persona 5, Royal has a myriad of additions and changes that I all loved. The new character, Kasumi ([spoiler]or her real name, Sumire[/spoiler]), turned out to be my favourite girl in the game, and the extended story and new palace were an amazing experience that elevated the ending. With the addition of the third semester, the party can receive third-tier personas, which are a great callback to the first awakenings and give a strong skill upgrade. The grappling hook is also an amazing addition, and I am a sucker for hook mechanics in games. Every palace has been altered to incorporate it and makes exploration more fun. The new will seeds located in palaces are sometimes hidden behind grapple points and make the flow of exploration more interesting as well. Another addition that stood out to me and improved battles was Showtime Attacks that deal massive damage along with a fun and flashy animation to go along with it.

I only have a few complaints with this game, and even then it is not really a huge deal. While I think the story is mostly well paced, the start is a bit slow even if it does a good job of establishing things. I did not really mind it that much on my first playthrough, but upon replays it felt a bit like a slog. My other complaint is that I kind of find mementos boring. Sometimes I like it because it is a great place to easily level up to max with Ryuji's instant kill ability and lets me fight again before another palace in the story, but it is a slog to complete all stamps for Jose because they're randomized. The map is also designed like Persona 3's Tartarus, which is kind of mediocre and uninteresting, but at least it helps me partially shut my brain off and watch something on the side while I collect all stamps. Lastly, I think merciless difficulty is way too easy. Due to the damage multiplier, I can kill most things with one attack aimed at a weakness, and it provides less of a challenge than hard difficulty.

In the end, Persona 5 Royal is a fantastic game that I would highly recommend to both JRPG fans and beginners looking to get into one or experience some more. Despite Persona 4 being my favourite, Persona 5 clearly has the best gameplay and is the easiest difficulty-wise. Even though I am the type to start or recommend things in release order, Persona 5 is self-contained like Persona 3 and 4, and since it is well refined, I can comfortably recommend this as a good starting point.

I haven't even beaten this game yet, and I can already tell you that this game is life changing. I find myself almost rushing through the combat sections just so I can sit in my room and make tools with my cat (or not cat?). I put off playing this for so long due to the length. I had no idea that I'd be only a little halfway through and already thinking about a potential next playthrough. 

If you're like me and putting this off because it's "too long" - just play it. You won't regret it.
Great game, no time wasted at all  also it hurts that it ended and i dont want it to end but every story must end 10/10 would do it again

Persona 5 Royal is great, It's one of the games that you need to experience at least once in your life. 

Also, it's one of my favourite games of all time and Honestly, I don't know if I liked Persona or Yakuza more.

The Good:

Stunning Soundtrack: Honestly, some of the best music I’ve ever heard in a game.

Gameplay Loop: The seamless transition between daily life and high-stakes action is brilliant.

Multiple Endings: The variety here is spot on and adds real weight to your choices.

Story & Characters: A solid historical feel with good character arcs that stay engaging throughout.

The Bad:

Pacing Issues: Given the length, the character development could’ve been even deeper.

Filler Dialogue: Some conversations are a bit of a slog and don’t add much, though luckily you can skip them.

All in all this is a great game that if you're even remotely curious about you should play it
Best UI transition, Best Music, Best Life Simulation. Absolute PEAK Games!!
A phenominal game with good story, gameplay and musics. 10/10 would play it again.
hate that ♥♥♥♥ ass cat and the fact you even have the option to date futaba. 10/10 game

I genuinely don't know what to say about this game. It's such an utter masterpiece in art, music, story and even the combat is really damn good - I don't even like turn-based RPGs that much! Honestly, I only have 2 complaints - I don't even think they're uncommon though. The 5th major boss and the final major boss (If you're going down the Royal path) were much more difficult than any other portion of the game - Including the Reaper, which is quite odd. Other than that, I genuinely love this game and, at the time of writing, I think it's now my favorite game of all time.

This is the sequel to my favourite game ever made, so I'm not going to bother with any prelude here.

P5 does a really good job blending stealth mechanics into the modern Persona formula. It can make you feel like a real phantom thief, which already makes this a standout game. The battling system is also fantastic, both expanding on what P3 & P4 did as well as adding new features entirely.

There's only two real complaints I have about this game: firstly, some of it's novel additions aren't explored very well. For example, the games combo system is only used in a tutorial, and is basically optional until you face one of the mid game bosses. This makes for a really irritating difficulty curve, and was genuinely the only part of this game I couldn't enjoy.

Secondly, much of the content added in Royal over the original P5 is in the third semester. The requirements are quite strict, and some of them are never mentioned or implied to the player. If you miss out on it, you have to spend another ~70 hours on a second playthrough without failing any of the conditions. Very unappreciated.

Apart from that, all the normal pros and cons of modernsona apply.
It can play my pc in low to medium setting and the game is good huge upgrade after playing p4g
This is probably the best story game I have ever played, I HIGHLY recommend this to anyone, it will change your life. I promise ;)

Not as interesting a story as p3, and not as interesting of characters as p4, but the gameplay is significantly better than either. The bonus content also feels worthwhile (unlike the "golden" part of p4g).

Honestly, I went into this game thinking I wouldn’t like it. I haven’t played a turn-based game and actually liked it before, but this game is special. The story is rich, and the interactions between the characters are so enjoyable and entertaining. I 100% recommend this game to others.

(I also faced some issues with my frames suddenly stuttering while playing the game. A quick fix for this issue is turning the NVIDIA overlay off.)

This game is so cool, even navigating the menus is cool.  As a web developer who has to present information and interactive elements across different screen sizes while keeping everything organized and uncluttered, the UI in [b]Persona 5[/b] is something to study.  How did they get away with breaking all the rules?  What kind of CSS are they writing to animate all of this while still keeping it so clean?

Beyond gushing over the UI, the rest of the game's presentation is excellent too.  Having been to Japan - although not Tokyo specifically for very long - the city environments, train stations, and underground malls all felt very familiar.  Even the confusing [i]multiple exits leading to street level at different elevations[/i] felt accurate to my experience.  The Metaverse palaces are equally impressive.  There are nine fixed story-related palaces alongside the randomly generated Mementos dungeon, and every single one feels distinct and memorable - from the environments and enemies to the puzzles and how they tie into the story.  None of them felt dull, rushed, or copy/pasted.  Even after the game establishes a familiar formula, it somehow keeps everything feeling fresh.  Charging into a palace while "Life Will Change" plays never got old.  Funny enough, that song had already been on one of my workout playlists long before I played the game, so I subconsciously experienced it backwards - the game was playing my workout music every time I went into a boss fight.

The gameplay itself lives up to the presentation.  Like any turn-based RPG, it throws a lot of systems and rules at you initially.  But I quickly settled into a rhythm, learned enemy strengths and weaknesses, and got comfortable - right before the game throws something new at me and forces me to adapt again.  It's an excellent combat system.  And the non-combat systems are meaningful too.  Spending time with friends and progressing their individual storylines matters because it unlocks useful gameplay perks.  The same goes for side activities like darts and pool, which are surprisingly well executed.  All of these systems reinforce each other and come together into a really cohesive experience.

That said, I do have some qualms with the game.  I struggled to stay on the same tempo as it at times.  The tutorial section goes on forever.  The slower social segments would sometimes drag when I wanted more combat, while the dungeon crawling and boss fights could occasionally become exhausting in the opposite direction.  The calendar system also means you can't always progress exactly what you want when you want to.  I originally started the game because of a friend's recommendation, and they were genuinely upset with how I played.  You're supposed to invest in certain social stats immediately and keep a guide open at all times to min-max efficiency.  The game also hands you cracked Personas right from the start that I was [i]"not supposed"[/i] to use.  That mentality kind of soured parts of the experience for me, even though I still enjoyed the game overall because everything is just so stylish and well executed.

The additions from [b]Persona 5 Royal[/b] specifically also felt a little funny from a narrative perspective.  We go from fighting the [spoiler]God of Control[/spoiler] at the end of the original game to fighting a [spoiler]cognitive scientist[/spoiler] in the Royal expansion?  But because of gameplay power scaling, the latter has to be a higher level.  Don't get me wrong - the Royal content itself is great.  The characters, story additions, and palace design are all excellent.  It's just amusing for me to think about.
